name: Warne Marsh
abstract: Warne Marion Marsh (October 26, 1927 – December 18, 1987) was an American tenor saxophonist. Born in Los Angeles, his playing first came to prominence in the 1950s as a protégé of pianist Lennie Tristano and earned attention in the 1970s as a member of Supersax.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Buddy Rich and His Killer Force/ Lee Konitz-Warne Marsh Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Warne and I would pick it up phrase by phrase as he played it and learn the whole tune before writing it down
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t think there’s any one thing I got, except understanding more about Lee Konitz and Warne Marsh
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
